description: Schema for a v2 IDL module YAML file. Each module groups related types and functions for one area of the C API.

module.description: Module name. Used in the generated header as a section comment.

handles.description: Opaque handle types, keyed by name. Always rendered as pointer typedefs (e.g. typedef void* name_ptr).

callbacks.description: Callback function pointer typedefs, keyed by name. Always rendered with _cb suffix.

aliases.description: Named type aliases, keyed by unprefixed name. The generator prepends the version prefix and appends the aliases suffix from metadata.

structs.description: Struct definitions with consumer-visible fields, keyed by struct name.

enums.description: Enum definitions, keyed by enum name.

constants.description: Named constants, keyed by constant name.

error_groups.description: Grouped error code definitions, keyed by category name.

functions.description: Function definitions, keyed by C function name.

StatusEntry.description: A single lifecycle event: [state, version, date]. State is one of: unstable, stable, frozen, deprecated, removed.

HandleDefinition.description: An opaque handle type. Consumers don't know the internal structure — just a pointer.

cleanup_with.description: Name of the function that destroys/frees this handle.

status.description: Lifecycle history stack, newest-first. Top entry is current status.

CallbackDefinition.description: A callback function pointer typedef (e.g. typedef void (*name_cb)(args)).

return_pointer.description: Pointer indirection level on the return type.

return_const.description: Whether the return type is const-qualified.

parameters.description: Map of parameter name to definition.

status.description: Lifecycle history stack, newest-first. Top entry is current status.

AliasDefinition.description: A named type alias. Maps a name to an underlying type (primitive or declared).

underlying.description: The type this aliases. A primitive name or another declared type name.

qualified.description: If true, emitted verbatim — no prefix or suffix applied. The key IS the C name. Use for non-prefixed types (e.g. sel_t, idx_t).

status.description: Lifecycle history stack, newest-first. Top entry is current status.

Function.description: A C API function. Every function returns an error code by default and takes typed parameters.

summary.description: One-line description of what the function does. Used in the generated doc comment.

role.description: Semantic role of the function relative to the type it belongs_to.

belongs_to.description: The type this function operates on. Used for grouping and code generation.

parameters.description: Map of parameter name to definition. Order is preserved and determines the signature.

return_type.description: Return type name. Defaults to duckdb_error_code (the v2 convention).

return_pointer.description: Pointer indirection level on the return type. 0 = value, 1 = pointer, etc.

return_const.description: Whether the return type is const-qualified.

added.description: API version when this function was introduced.

deprecated.description: API version when this function was deprecated.

status.description: Lifecycle history stack, newest-first. Top entry is current status.

type.description: Type name — a primitive or a declared type.

indirection.description: Pointer indirection level. 0 = pass by value, 1 = pointer, 2 = pointer-to-pointer.

kind.description: Parameter kind — combines direction (in/out) and ownership. 'IN' = input, callee does not retain. 'IN_TRANSFER' = input, callee takes ownership (caller must not free). 'OUT' = output, caller must free/destroy the result. 'OUT_BORROW' = output, callee retains ownership (caller must not free; valid only as long as owning object lives). Only 'OUT' / 'OUT_BORROW' require indirection >= 1.

StructDefinition.description: A C struct with consumer-visible fields. Use handles for opaque types instead.

pointer_alias.description: If true, generate a typedef pointer alias (e.g. typedef struct_name* struct_name_ptr).

status.description: Lifecycle history stack, newest-first. Top entry is current status.

StructField.description: A single field within a struct.

type.description: Type name — a primitive or declared type.

array_size.description: If set, render the field as an inline fixed-size array of this length (e.g. `char name[N];`). Mutually exclusive with `pointer > 0`.

EnumDefinition.description: A C enum typedef. Values are auto-numbered from 0 unless explicitly set.

values.description: Map of enum member name to value/description. Order determines auto-numbering.

status.description: Lifecycle history stack, newest-first. Top entry is current status.

EnumValue.description: A single enum member. Omit 'value' to auto-number sequentially.

value.description: Explicit integer value. If omitted, continues from the previous member.
Constant value — an integer or string expression.

ErrorGroup.description: A group of related error codes. The full 32-bit code is (group_id << 16) | code.

group_id.description: Group identifier (upper 16 bits of the error code).

entries.description: Error entries within this group, keyed by error code name.

ErrorEntry.description: A single error code within an error group.

code.description: Error code identifier (lower 16 bits of the error code).
